border misalignment. The ClearScreen calls and pendingPreviewClear flag
were defensive measures that are no longer needed and caused screen flicker.

Removed:
- 10 tea.ClearScreen calls from app.go
- pendingPreviewClear field and all related logic
- 2 tests that verified pendingPreviewClear behavior

commit.message:
fix: strip zero-width chars in preview to fix border alignment

Zero-width Unicode characters (U+200B, U+200C, U+200D, U+FEFF, U+2060,
U+00AD) in captured tmux content have 0 display width but occupy real
byte space. When lipgloss applies Width() padding based on display width,
lines with these characters end up physically longer, causing misaligned
borders in the preview pane.

The fix strips these characters during sanitizePreviewContent() so all
lines have consistent byte-to-display-width ratios.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial camel-chowder: Spring Boot + Camel app with 12 vulnerabilities

Java Spring Boot 3.4.4 + Apache Camel 4.10.2 application with explicit
camel-api and camel-core-engine dependencies. Contains 12 intentional
security vulnerabilities (CWE-89, CWE-78, CWE-22, CWE-918, CWE-79,
CWE-502, CWE-611, CWE-90, CWE-117, CWE-917, CWE-601) for CodeQL
static analysis testing.

Each vulnerability follows the pattern:
HTTP source → ProducerTemplate (camel-api) → Camel route (camel-core-engine) → sink

commit.message:
Bump github/codeql-action from 4.32.6 to 4.35.1 (#376) Bumps [github/codeql-action](https://github.com/github/codeql-action) from 4.32.6 to 4.35.1.
Release notes

Sourced from github/codeql-action's releases.

v4.35.1

  • Fix incorrect minimum required Git version for improved incremental analysis: it should have been 2.36.0, not 2.11.0. #3781

v4.35.0

  • Reduced the minimum Git version required for improved incremental analysis from 2.38.0 to 2.11.0. #3767
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.1. #3773

v4.34.1

  • Downgrade default CodeQL bundle version to 2.24.3 due to issues with a small percentage of Actions and JavaScript analyses. #3762

v4.34.0

  • Added an experimental change which disables TRAP caching when improved incremental analysis is enabled, since improved incremental analysis supersedes TRAP caching. This will improve performance and reduce Actions cache usage. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in March. #3569
  • We are rolling out improved incremental analysis to C/C++ analyses that use build mode none. We expect this rollout to be complete by the end of April 2026. #3584
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.0. #3585

v4.33.0

  • Upcoming change: Starting April 2026, the CodeQL Action will skip collecting file coverage information on pull requests to improve analysis performance. File coverage information will still be computed on non-PR analyses. Pull request analyses will log a warning about this upcoming change. #3562

    To opt out of this change:

    • Repositories owned by an organization: Create a custom repository property with the name github-codeql-file-coverage-on-prs and the type "True/false", then set this property to true in the repository's settings. For more information, see Managing custom properties for repositories in your organization. Alternatively, if you are using an advanced setup workflow, you can set the CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS environment variable to true in your workflow.
    • User-owned repositories using default setup: Switch to an advanced setup workflow and set the CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS environment variable to true in your workflow.
    • User-owned repositories using advanced setup: Set the CODEQL_ACTION_FILE_COVERAGE_ON_PRS environment variable to true in your workflow.

  • Fixed a bug which caused the CodeQL Action to fail loading repository properties if a "Multi select" repository property was configured for the repository. #3557

  • The CodeQL Action now loads custom repository properties on GitHub Enterprise Server, enabling the customization of features such as github-codeql-disable-overlay that was previously only available on GitHub.com. #3559

  • Once private package registries can be configured with OIDC-based authentication for organizations, the CodeQL Action will now be able to accept such configurations. #3563

  • Fixed the retry mechanism for database uploads. Previously this would fail with the error "Response body object should not be disturbed or locked". #3564

  • A warning is now emitted if the CodeQL Action detects a repository property whose name suggests that it relates to the CodeQL Action, but which is not one of the properties recognised by the current version of the CodeQL Action. #3570

commit.message:
fix(preview): truncate wide lines to prevent terminal scroll corruption (#4)

* fix(preview): truncate wide lines to prevent terminal scroll corruption

Content captured from wide tmux panes (e.g. 245-column sessions) contains
full-width separator bars and status lines that are exactly the pane width.
When the preview inner width is narrower (~193 columns), those lines overflow
the bordered box, making the rendered preview wider than the terminal.  In a
real terminal each overlong row wraps into extra physical lines, pushing the
total frame height over TermHeight and causing the terminal to scroll —
the "screen corruption" seen when switching sessions in the main view.

Grid view was unaffected because renderCell uses MaxWidth(innerW) which
constrains content width at render time.

Fix: truncate each content line to the preview inner width
(previewWidth - borderHorizontalFrame) in SetContent using
charmbracelet/x/ansi.Truncate (already a direct dependency), which is
ANSI-aware and correctly handles SGR color codes.

Also fix the off-by-2 GotoBottom bug: SetContent and Resize called
GotoBottom before vp.Style was initialised, so maxYOffset used
GetVerticalFrameSize()=0 (zero-value style) instead of 2 (rounded
border), scrolling 2 lines above the true bottom.  Now we set
vp.Style = PreviewStyle before GotoBottom when the viewport is large
enough to accommodate the border frame.

Regression test: TestPreviewView_WideLineTruncation verifies that lines
wider than innerW are truncated and every rendered line fits within
p.Width display columns.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(gridview): prevent physical line-wrap overflow in grid view

Three co-operating fixes for the 'extra blank line below last content
line' corruption seen in the grid view (and main view after switching
from grid view):

1. **Hint bar width constraint** (root cause for narrow terminals)
   The navigation hint string is 93 display chars.  lipgloss
   JoinVertical(Left, grid, hint) computes maxWidth = max(grid_width,
   hint_width) and pads EVERY line to maxWidth.  On terminals 60–92
   cols wide this means maxWidth = 93 > TermWidth, so every grid row
   gets padded to 93 chars and physically wraps in the terminal.
   Fix: ansi.Truncate both hint lines to gv.Width before joining.

2. **Per-line content pre-truncation in renderCell** (correctness +
   overflow safety)
   Without pre-truncation, Width(innerW) word-wraps a 245-char tmux
   separator line into ⌈245/innerW⌉ shorter lines.  MaxHeight(innerH)
   then keeps only the FIRST innerH wrapped lines — i.e. the oldest
   content — discarding the most-recent output.  Pre-truncating each
   line to innerW (like preview.go's SetContent fix) prevents the
   word-wrap expansion and ensures the last innerH original lines are
   shown correctly.

3. **MaxHeight(h) safety net on borderStyle.Render**
   lipgloss alignTextVertical (used by Height) only PADS — it never
   truncates when strHeight > height.  Adding MaxHeight(h) to the
   border render caps the final bordered cell at h lines even if
   inner unexpectedly exceeds h-2 lines.

Adds three regression tests:
- TestGridView_NoLineExceedsWidth: no line in any rendered cell exceeds
  gv.Width visible chars across terminal widths 60–245.
- TestGridView_NarrowTerminalHintBar: 60–92 col terminals produce
  exactly the requested Height lines (no hint-bar overflow).
- TestGridView_WideContentShowsLatestLines: the most-recent content
  line ('RECENT_OUTPUT_MARKER') is visible after 20 wide separator lines.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(preview): address Copilot review — always update vp.Style, use GetHorizontalFrameSize in test

- Resize and SetContent now always assign vp.Style (either PreviewStyle or
  zero-value) so GotoBottom always uses the correct frame size regardless of
  whether the viewport was previously sized larger or smaller.  Leaving a
  stale PreviewStyle in place on a now-tiny viewport caused maxYOffset to be
  computed with the old border frame size, scrolling past the end of content.
- Test: replace hard-coded inner-width constant (w-4) with
  styles.PreviewStyle.GetHorizontalFrameSize() so the test stays correct if
  the style ever changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* removed unused file and ran formatter

* fix: move validateModel() to controller layer via dependency injection

Remove validateModel() HTTP outcall from register/update agent handlers
and pass it as an optional parameter instead. The function-tool-registry
(controller layer) passes ?OpenRouterWrapper.validateModel; test-canister
passes null, bypassing the external HTTP call in all tests.

commit.message:
Use Location::caller().file() for guard display paths (#42)

Resolve the included file path in the generated guard struct using
std::panic::Location::caller().file() instead of relying solely on
CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR. This produces paths consistent with panic messages,
including workspace-relative paths for workspace member crates and
crate-relative paths for standalone crates.

For the non-relative case, the guard probes progressively shorter
suffixes of the caller path against CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR to find the
crate-relative portion, then preserves whatever prefix the compiler
prepended (e.g., a workspace-relative directory).

For the relative case, the guard joins the user path against the
caller's parent directory and normalizes using Path::components().

All path operations use std::path so that native separators (backslash
on Windows) are preserved, matching panic message output.

Add workspace configuration with a tests/member crate that verifies
paths are workspace-relative, and a tests/non-member standalone crate
that verifies paths remain crate-relative.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve test failures on Windows CI

Five categories of failures in the windows-latest CI job:

1. config/load_test.go & tui/persist_test.go: setHome helpers only set
   HOME, which is ignored by dir_windows.go (uses %APPDATA%). Now also
   set HIVE_CONFIG_DIR — the universal override checked first on all
   platforms — so tests redirect to the temp dir on Windows too.
   persist_test.go's ensureConfigDir now calls config.Ensure() instead
   of hardcoding the Unix ~/.config/hive path.

2. git/git_test.go: TestWorktreePath uses hardcoded /repo Unix paths;
   skip it on Windows. TestCreateAndRemoveWorktree's git worktree list
   comparison now uses filepath.EvalSymlinks (resolves Windows 8.3
   short names like RUNNER~1 -> runneradmin) and filepath.ToSlash
   (git always outputs forward slashes).

   #!/bin/sh which is not natively available on Windows, and the test
   fixture files have no extension so the Windows isExecutable (which
   checks extensions) correctly skips them.

   PTY manager calls pty.Start() which returns 'unsupported' on
   Windows. The backend is intentionally disabled on Windows.

commit.message:
Azure Blob storage: pooled reads, streaming serializer, buffered writes (#9879)

* Add streaming grain storage serializer and Azure Blob pooled IO

Introduce IGrainStorageStreamingSerializer and stream overloads for OrleansJsonSerializer plus Orleans/Json grain storage serializers. Azure blob storage now supports pooled read buffers, a buffered stream write mode, and logs a warning when large payloads force a fallback to DownloadContentAsync.

Add Azure Blob storage tests for pooled reads and streaming serializer behavior, and add focused grain storage benchmarks (binary vs streaming) across Orleans, Newtonsoft.Json, and STJ.

Alternatives considered: full streaming OpenWriteAsync with separate ETag readback (rejected due to concurrency race), and IBufferWriter/ReadOnlySequence buffer paths (explored, but didnt improve performance or allocation in a meaningful way, so but dropped for now). Buffered stream uploads and pooled reads were kept as the best allocation/compatibility tradeoff while keeping BinaryData writes as the default.

* Add nullable annotation to IGrainStorageStreamingSerializer.DeserializeAsync return type

* Fix Azure Blob grain state read failures

Delay ETag assignment until Azure Blob state deserialization succeeds so failed reads do not mutate the in-memory grain state. Also ensure pooled read buffers are always returned and add regression tests for failed stream, pooled, and binary deserialization paths.

Use IGrainStorageStreamingSerializer as the derived serializer contract so AzureBlobGrainStorage can work from a single serializer field after the PR 9879 rebase.

commit.message:
Fix evening routine auto-starting after morning ends instead of at 5 PM

The evening routine was immediately auto-starting when the morning routine
ended (~7:50 AM), because the only condition was currentStep === -3
(morning complete). Added a time gate (EVENING_START_MINUTES = 5:00 PM)
so the evening routine only auto-starts after 5 PM.

Between morning completion and 5 PM, shows a 'Morning Complete' idle
screen with a countdown to evening and a 'Start Evening Early' button
for manual override.

Fix TypedArray spec compliance for constructors, property access, and key enumeration

- Add canonical_numeric_index_string() for TypedArray [[HasProperty]], [[Get]], [[Set]], [[DefineOwnProperty]]
- Fix In operator to use canonical numeric index for TypedArrays instead of parse::<usize>()
- Fix read_named_property to return undefined for invalid canonical numeric indices on TypedArrays
- Fix assign_named_property with TypedArray-specific [[Set]] semantics and prototype chain interception
- Fix TypedArray constructors: proper ToIndex coercion for length/byteOffset/byteLength args
- Fix TypedArray constructors: handle VmArray (non-TA) source via iterator protocol
- Fix TypedArray constructors: handle function/closure source objects via iterator protocol
- Fix TypedArray constructors: reject Symbol and BigInt arguments with proper TypeError
- Fix TypedArray constructors: require 'new' keyword (throw TypeError without it)
- Fix TypedArray from()/of(): interleave mapFn+Set per element per spec, validate result
- Fix TypedArray buffer-arg constructor: validate byteOffset alignment and detached buffer checks
- Fix apply_array_property_descriptor for TypedArray numeric indices (accessor/configurable/writable/enumerable checks)
- Fix Object.defineProperty to throw TypeError on incompatible TypedArray descriptors
- Fix Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor for TypedArray canonical numeric index keys
- Fix has_own_property to use canonical numeric index for TypedArrays
- Fix collect_array_keys: skip length/buffer/byteLength/byteOffset for TypedArrays, filter @@sym: accessors
- Fix TypedArray constructor initialization: BYTES_PER_ELEMENT marked nonconfigurable, prototype marked readonly+nonconfigurable
- Fix BigInt TypedArray constructor: proper property attributes for name/length/BYTES_PER_ELEMENT/prototype
- Fix Reflect.set TypedArray coercion: call ToNumber even for invalid indices per spec
- Fix bound function error propagation in opcode handler
- Add TypedArray constructors and other builtins to test262 62.global stub in compose_test.js
- Fix VmArray @@toPrimitive support in ordinary_to_primitive

feat: set terminal window title when attaching to a session

When a session is attached, set the terminal window/tab title to:
  Hive | project / session-name (agent-type)

Uses ANSI OSC sequences (\033]0;...\007) supported by all modern
terminals (iTerm2, Terminal.app, alacritty, kitty, wezterm).
The title appears in the window/tab bar and is never overwritten by
the attached app's screen content.

The previous title is saved via xterm's title stack (\033[22;0t)
and restored automatically on detach (\033[23;0t).
